text_fr: At  its  933rd  meeting,  on  13  February  1961,  following the  announcement  of the  death  of Mr.  Lumumba,  the Council  decided,  under  rule  33,  paragraph  3,  of  the provisional  rules  of procedure,  to  adjourn  the  meeting for  forty-eight  hours.
text_en: At its 933rd meeting, on 13 February 1961, following the announcement of the death of Mr. Lumumba, the Council decided, under rule 33, paragraph 3, of the provisional rules of procedure, to adjourn the meeting for strong-eight hours.

text_fr: Adopted  by JO votes to 1 ( Liberia).
text_en: Adopted by JO votes to 1 (Liberia).

text_fr: 161  (1961).  Resolution  of 21  February  1961 [S/4741]
text_en: 161 (1961). Resolution of 21 February 1961 [S/4741]

text_fr: A
text_en: A

text_fr: The  Security  Council,
text_en: The Security Council,

text_fr: Having  considered the  situation  in  the  Congo,
text_en: Having considered the situation in the Congo,

text_fr: Having  learnt  with  deep  regret of the  announcement of  the  killing  of  the  Congolese  leaders, Mr. Patrice Lumumba,  Mr.  Maurice Mpolo and  Mr.  Joseph Okito,
text_en: Having learned with deep regret of the announcement of the killing of the Congolese leaders, Mr. Patrice Lumumba, Mr. Maurice Mpolo and Mr. Joseph Okito,

text_fr: Deeply  concerned at  the  grave  repercussions  of these crimes  and  the  danger  of  widespread  civil  war  and bloodshed in  the  Congo  and  the  threat  to  international peace  and  security,
text_en: Deeply concerned at the grave repercussions of these crimes and the danger of widepread civil war and flooded in the Congo and the threat to international peace and security,

text_fr: Noting the  report  of the  Secretary-General's Special Representative,  dated  12  February  1961, 3 bringing  to light  the  development  of a  serious  civil  war  situation and  preparations  therefor,
text_en: Noting the report of the Secretary-General's Special Representative, dated 12 February 1961, 3 stranding to light the development of a serious civil war situation and preparations therefor,

items_fr: 1. Urges that  the  United  Nations  take  immediately all  appropriate  measures  to  prevent  the  occurrence  of civil  war  in  the  Congo,  including  arrangements  for cease-fires,  the  halting  of  all  military  operations,  the prevention  of clashes,  and the  use  of force,  if necessary, in  the  last  resort;

2. Urges that  measures  be  taken  for  the  immediate withdrawal  and  evacuation  from  the  Congo  of all  Bel› gian  and  other  foreign  military  and  paramilitary  per› sonnel  and  political  advisers  not  under  the  United Nations  Command,  and  mercenaries;

3. Calls  upon all  States  to take immediate and energe› tic  measures  to prevent the  departure  of such  personnel for  the  Congo  from  their territories,  and  for  the  denial of transit  and other facilities  to  them;

4. Decides that  an  immediate  and  impartial  investi› gation  be  held  in  order  to  ascertain  the  circumstances of the  death  of Mr.  Lumumba  and  his  colleagues  and that the  perpetrators of these  crimes  be  punished;

5. Reaffirms Security  Council  resolutions  143  (1960) of 14  July  1960,  14"5  (1960)  of 22  July  1960  and  146 (1960)  of 9  August  1960  and  General Assembly  resolu› tion  1474  (ES-IV)  of 20  September  1960  and  reminds all  States  of their  obligations  under  these  resolutions.
items_en: 1. Urges that the United Nations take immediate all appropriate measures to prevent the occurrence of civil war in the Congo, including arrangements for cease-fires, the stopping of all military operations, the prevention of clashes, and the use of force, if necessary, in the last resort;

2. Urges that measures be taken for the immediate withdrawal and evacuation from the Congo of all Bel› gian and other foreign military and paramilitary per› sonnel and political advisers not under the United Nations Command, and mercenaries;

3. Calls upon all States to take immediate and energe› tick measures to prevent the departure of such personnel for the Congo from their territories, and for the denial of transit and other facilities to them;

4. Decisions that an immediate and impartial investment› delegation be kept in order to secure the circumstances of the death of Mr. Lumumba and his colleagues and that the perpetrators of these crimes be punished;

5. Reaffirmations Security Council resolutions 143 (1960) of 14 July 1960, 14"5 (1960) of 22 July 1960 and 146 (1960) of 9 August 1960 and General Assembly resolved›tion 1474 (ES-IV) of 20 September 1960 and recall all States of their obligations under these resolutions.

text_fr: A  sa 933e  seance,  le  13  fevrier  1961,  le  Conseil,  apres avoir appris la  mort de  M.  Lumumba, a  decide,  confor› mement  a l'alinea c de  !'article  33  de  son  reglement interieur  provisoire,  d'ajourner la  seance  au  surlende› main.
text_en: At its 933rd session, on 13 February 1961, the Council, after learning of the death of Mr Lumumba, decided, in accordance with Article 33 of its provisional internal rules, to postpone the sitting on the left-hand side.

text_fr: Adoptee par JO voix contre une ( Liberia).
text_en: Adopted by OJ vote against one (Liberia).

text_fr: 161  (1961).  Resolution  du  21  fevrier  1961 [S/4741]
text_en: 161 (1961). Resolution of 21 February 1961 [S/4741]

text_fr: A
text_en: A

text_fr: Le  Conseil  de  securite,
text_en: The Security Council,

text_fr: Ayant  examine la  situation  au  Congo,
text_en: Having examined the situation in the Congo,

text_fr: Ayant  appris  avec  un  profond  regret la  nouvelle  du meurtre  des  dirigeants  congolais,  M.  Patrice  Lumumba, M.  Maurice  Mpolo  et  M.  Joseph  Okito,
text_en: Having learned with deep regret the news of the murder of the Congolese leaders, Mr. Patrice Lumumba, Mr. Maurice Mpolo and Mr. Joseph Okito,

text_fr: Profondement  preoccupe par  les  graves  repercussions de  ces  crimes  et  par  le  risque  d'une  guerre  civile  et d'effusions  de  sang  generalisees  au  Congo,  ainsi  que par la  menace  a la  paix  et  a la  securite  internationales,
text_en: Deeply concerned by the serious repercussions of these crimes and the risk of civil war and generalized bloodshed in the Congo, as well as by the threat to international peace and security,

text_fr: Notant le  rapport  du  representant  special  du  Secre› taire  general,  date  du  12  fevrier  1961 3 , qui  expose !'apparition d'une  grave  situation  de  guerre  civile  et Jes  preparatifs  faits  a cette  fin,
text_en: Noting the report of the Special Representative of the Secretary General, dated 12 February 1961, 3 , which sets out a serious situation of civil war and preparations made for this purpose,

items_fr: I. Demande  instamment que  les  Nations  Unies  pren› nent  immediatement  toutes  mesures  appropriees  pour empecher le  declenchement d 'une guerre civile au Congo, notamment  des  dispositions  concernant  des  cessez-le› feu, la  cessation  de  toutes  operations  militaires,  la prevention de  combats et le  recours a la force,  si  besoin est,  en  dernier  ressort;

2. Demande  instamment que des  mesures  soient prises pour  le  retrait  et  !'evacuation  immediate  du  Congo de  tous Jes  personnels militaire et paramili~aire et conseil› Iers  politiques  belges  et d'autres nationalites  ne  relevant pas  du  Commandement  des  Nations  Unies,  ainsi  que des  mercenaires;

3. Prie tous  les  Etats  de  prendre  immediatement  des mesures  energiques  pour  empecher  sur  leur  territoire le  depart de ces  personnels pour le  Congo et leur refuser le passage  et  autres  facilites;

4. Decide qu'une enquete  impartiale  aura lieu -imme› diatement  en  vue  de  determiner  les  circonstances de la  mort  de  M.  Lumumba et  de  ses  collegues  et  que  les auteurs  de  ces  crimes  seront  chaties;

5. Reaffirme les  resolutions  143  (1960), 145 (1960) et 146(1960) du Conseil de securite, en date des 14juillet, 22  juillet  et  9  aout  1960,  et  la  resolution  1474  (ES-IV) de  l'Assemblee generale,  en  date  du 20  septembre  1960, et rappelle  a tous  Jes  Etats  leurs  obligations  aux  termes de  ces  resolutions.
items_en: I. Urges the United Nations immediately to take all appropriate measures to prevent the outbreak of a civil war in the Congo, including provisions concerning ceasefires, cessation of all military operations, prevention of fighting and the use of force, if necessary, as a last resort;

2. Urges that measures be taken for the immediate withdrawal and evacuation from the Congo of all military and paramilitary personnel and advisers› Belgian and other nationalist policies outside the United Nations Command, as well as mercenaries;
3. Requests all States to take immediate and energetic measures to prevent the departure of these personnel for the Congo on their territory and to deny them passage and other facilities;

4. Decides that an impartial investigation shall take place -imme› diatoire to determine the circumstances of the death of Mr. Lumumba and his colleagues and that the perpetrators of these crimes shall be punished;

5. Reaffirms Security Council Resolutions 143 (1960), 145 (1960) and 146 (1960) of 14 July, 22 July and 9 August 1960, and General Assembly Resolution 1474 (ES-IV) of 20 September 1960, and reminds all States of their obligations under these Resolutions.

text_fr: B
text_en: B

text_fr: The  Security  Council,
text_en: The Security Council,

text_fr: Gravely  concerned at  the  continuing  deterioration  of the  situation  in  the  Congo  and  at  the  prevalence  of conditions  which  seriously  imperil  peace  and  order and  the  unity  and  territorial  integrity  of  the  Congo, and threaten  international  peace  and security,
text_en: Gravely concerned at the continuing deterioration of the situation in the Congo and at the prevalence of conditions which seriously imperil peace and order and the unity and territorial integrity of the Congo, and threeen international peace and security,

text_fr: Noting with  deep  regret  and  concern the  systematic violations  of human  rights  and  fundamental  freedoms and the general absence of the rule  of law  in the Congo,
text_en: Noting with deep regret and concerning the systematic violations of human rights and fundamental freedom and the general absence of the rule of law in the Congo,

text_fr: Recognizing the  imperative  necessity  for  the  restora› tion of parliamentary institutions in the Congo in accord› ance with  the  fundamental  law  of the  country,  so  that the  will  of the  people  should  be  reflected  through  the freely  elected  Parliament,
text_en: Recognizing the imperative need for the restoration›tion of parliamentary institutions in the Congo in agreement›ance with the federal law of the country, so that the will of the people should be reflected through the freely elected Parliament,

text_fr: Convinced that  the  solution  of the  problem  of the Congo lies  in the  hands  of the  Congolese  people  them› selves  without  any  interference  from  outside,  and  that there can be  no solution without conciliation,
text_en: Convinced that the solution of the problem of the Congo lies in the hands of the Congolee people them› sells without any interference from outside, and that there can be no solution without conciliation,

text_fr: Convinced  further that  the  imposition  of  any  solu› tion,  including  the  formation  of  any  government  not based  on genuine  conciliation,  would,  far  from  settling any  issues,  greatly  enhance  the  dangers  of  conflict within  the  Congo  and  the  threat  to international peace and security,
text_en: Convinced further that the taxation of any solu›tion, including the formation of any government not based on genuine conciliation, would, far from setting any issues, highly enhanced the dangers of conflict within the Congo and the threats to international peace and security,

items_fr: 1. Urges the  convening  of  the  Parliament  and  the taking of necessary protective measures in that connexion;

2. Urges that  Congolese  armed  units  and  personnel should  be  reorganized  and  brought  under  discipline and  control,  and  arrangements  made  on  impartial and equitable bases  to  that end  and  with  a  view  to  the elimination  of any  possibility  of interference  by  such units and personnel in  the political life  of the  Congo;

3. Calls  upon all  States  to extend  their  full  co-opera› tion and assistance,  and  to  take  such  measures  as  may be  necessary on  their  part,  for  the  implementation  of the  present resolution.
items_en: 1. Urges the convening of the Parliament and the taking of necessary protective measures in that connection;

2. Urges that Congolese armed units and personnel should be reorganized and bought under discipline and control, and arrangements made on impartial and equitable bases to that end and with a view to the elimination of any possibility of interference by such units and personnel in the political life of the Congo;
3. Calls upon all States to extend their full co-opera›tion and assistance, and to take such measures as may be necessary on their part, for the implementation of the present resolution.

text_fr: Adopted at the  942nd meeting by 9 votes to none,  with 2 abs› tentions ( France, Union of Soviet  Socialist  Republics).
text_en: Adopted at the 942nd meeting by 9 votes to none, with 2 abs› tentions (France, Union of Soviet Socialist Republics).

text_fr: 169 (1961).  Resolution  of 24  November  1961 [S/5002)
text_en: 169 (1961). Resolution of 24 November 1961 [S/5002]

text_fr: The Security  Council,
text_en: The Security Council,

text_fr: Recalling its  resolutions  143  (1960)  of 14  July  1960, 145  (1960)  of 22  July  1960,  146  (1960)  of 9 August  1960 and  161  (1961)  of 21  February  1961,
text_en: Recalling its resolutions 143 (1960) of 14 July 1960, 145 (1960) of 22 July 1960, 146 (1960) of 9 August 1960 and 161 (1961) of 21 February 1961,

text_fr: Recalling further General  Assembly  resolutions  1474 (ES-IV), 1592 (XV), 1599 (XV),  1600 (XV) and 1601  (XV),
text_en: Recalling further General Assembly resolutions 1474 (ES-IV), 1592 (XV), 1599 (XV), 1600 (XV) and 1601 (XV),

text_fr: B
text_en: B

text_fr: Le  Conseil  de  securite,
text_en: The Security Council,

text_fr: Gravement  preoccupe par  la  deterioration  continue de  la situation au Congo et par !'existence de conditions qui  mettent  gravement  en  danger  la  paix  et  l'ordre, ainsi  que  l'unite  et  l'integrite  territoriale  du  Congo, et  menacent  la  paix  et  la  securite  intemationales,
text_en: Gravely concerned by the continuing deterioration of the situation in the Congo and by the existence of conditions which seriously endanger peace and order, as well as the unity and territorial integrity of the Congo, and threaten international peace and security,

text_fr: Notant  avec  un  regret  et  un  souci  profonds les  viola› tions systematiques des  droits de  l'homme et des  libertes fondamentales et l'absence generale de  legalite au Congo,
text_en: Noting with deep regret and concern the systemic violations of human rights and fundamental freedoms and the general absence of legality in the Congo,

text_fr: Reconnaissant la  necessite  imperieuse  de  restaurer les  institutions  parlementaires  au  Congo  conformement a la  Loi fondamentale  du pays,  de  fa~on  que  la  volonte de  la  population se  reflete  par l'intermediaire du  Parle› ment  librement  elu,
text_en: Recognizing the urgent need to restore parliamentary institutions in the Congo in accordance with the Basic Law of the country, so that the will of the population is reflected in the intermediary of the freely elected Parliament,

text_fr: Convaincu que  la  solution  du  probleme  du  Congo est  entre  les  mains  du  peuple  congolais  lui-meme,  a l'abri de  toute ingerence  de  l'exterieur, et qu'il ne  peut y  avoir  de  solution  sans  conciliation,
text_en: Convinced that the solution to the problem of the Congo is in the hands of the Congolese people themselves, free from any influence from outsiders, and that there can be no solution without conciliation,

text_fr: Convaincu  en outre que toute solution  imposee, y  compris  la  formation  de  tout  gouvernement  ne  resul› tant  pas  d 'une  conciliation  veritable,  loin  de  regler aucun  probleme,  augmenterait  grandement  les  dangers de  conflit  a l'interieur du  Congo  et  la  menace  a la  paix et  a la  securite  internationales,
text_en: Convinced further that any binding solution, including the formation of any Government, would so much not be the result of a genuine conciliation, far from resolving any problem, greatly increase the dangers of conflict within the Congo and the threat to international peace and security,

items_fr: l. Demande  instamment la  convocation  du  Parlement et  !'adoption des  mesures  de  protection  necessaires a cet  egard;

2. Demande  instamment que  les  unites  et  le  personnel armes  congolais  soient  reorganises  et  soumis  a  une discipline  et a un controle et  que  des  dispositions  soient prises sur des  bases  impartiales  et  equitables  a cette fin et  en  vue  d'eliminer  toute  possibilite  d'ingerence de  ces  unites  et  de  ce  personnel  dans  la  vie  politique du  Congo;

3. Prie tous  les  Etats  de  preter  leur  cooperation et  leur  assistance  entieres  et  de  prendre  les  mesures qui  peuvent  etre  necessaires  de  leur  part,  en  vue  de l'execution de  la  presente  resolution.
items_en: l. Urges the convening of Parliament and the adoption of the necessary protective measures in this regard;

2. Urges that Congolese arms units and personnel be reorganized and subject to discipline and control and that arrangements be made on an impartial and equitable basis for this purpose and with a view to eliminating any possibility of the leadership of Congolese arms units and personnel in the political life of the Congo;
3. Requests all States to provide their full cooperation and assistance and to take such measures as may be necessary on their part, with a view to the implementation of this resolution.

text_fr: Adoptee d la 942' seance  par 9 voix contre zero,  avec 2 abs› tentions ( France, Union des Republiques  socialistes  sovie› tiques).
text_en: Adopted by 9 votes to none, with 2 abs› tentions (France, Union of Soviet Socialist Republics› ticks).

text_fr: 169 (1961).  Resolution  du  24  novembre  1961 [S/5002)
text_en: 169 (1961). Resolution of 24 November 1961 [S/5002]

text_fr: Le Consei/  de  securite,
text_en: The Security/Consei,

text_fr: Rappe/ant ses  resolutions  143  (1960)  du  14 juillet 1960, 145  (1960)  du 22  juillet  1960,  146 (1960)  du 9 aofit (1960) et  161  (1961)  du  21  fevrier  1961,
text_en: Rappe/ant ses resolutions 143 (1960) of 14 July 1960, 145 (1960) of 22 July 1960, 146 (1960) of 9 aofit (1960) and 161 (1961) of 21 February 1961,

text_fr: Rappe/ant  en outre les resolutions 1474 (ES-IV), 1592 (XV), 1599 (XV), 1600 (XV) et  1601 (XV) de l 'Assemblee  generate,
text_en: Further to resolutions 1474 (ES-IV), 1592 (XV), 1599 (XV), 1600 (XV) and 1601 (XV) of the General Assembly,
